summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete for the new Sofi (gestion.viajadverdad.com) push integration. 7/7 pytest cases green, 1 intentionally skipped (real-push would create a permanent row in Sofi production — re-enable via SOFI_RUN_REAL_PUSH=1). Verified end-to-end against the live preview URL with the supplied admin cookie token. (a) GET /api/itineraries/{id} now surfaces sofi_trip_id / sofi_url / sofi_pushed_at and all three are null pre-push as required for the FE branching. (b) POST /api/itineraries/{id}/push-to-sofi with dry_run=true returns {job_id, status:running, dry_run:true}; polling GET /api/itineraries/push-to-sofi/{job_id} reached status=done in ~40s and the result payload contained ok=true, dry_run=true, a non-empty filled_fields list, and a base64 screenshot >1KB (PNG). (c) The dry-run did NOT stamp sofi_trip_id on the itinerary document (verified via fresh GET after the job finished). (d) POST on a non-existent itinerary_id returns 404 with Spanish 'Itinerario no encontrado'. (e) GET status on a non-existent job_id returns 404 with Spanish 'Job no encontrado'. (f) When an itinerary already has sofi_trip_id (simulated via DB stamp to avoid polluting Sofi prod), POST with dry_run=false returns 409 Conflict with Spanish detail mentioning the existing trip number; POST with dry_run=true on the SAME pushed itinerary is still allowed (200 + running). (g) Real push (dry_run=false) test is wired but skipped by default — set SOFI_RUN_REAL_PUSH=1 to execute. Test data is auto-cleaned after the class (sofi_trip_id stamp removed via teardown).

minor.issue: Body parsing uses `payload.get('dry_run') or False`, which coerces an explicit `dry_run=0`/`dry_run=null`/`dry_run=''` to False but ALSO silently treats any truthy non-bool (e.g. the string 'false' from a misbehaving client) as True. Use `bool(payload.get('dry_run', False))` after a strict isinstance check, or move the body to a Pydantic model with `dry_run: bool = False`. Low impact today (the modal only sends booleans) but worth tightening before more callers appear.

Authorisation check `job['created_by'] != user.email and user.role != 'admin'` raises 403, but the SAME job_id with the wrong owner cannot be distinguished from a non-existent job from the client side (404 vs 403). Consider returning 404 for both to avoid leaking the existence of other agents' jobs.

No rate-limiting / no per-user concurrent-job guard. A user could fire 5 dry-runs in a loop; the global Playwright semaphore in sofi.py serialises them but each still consumes a sofi_push_jobs row and a worker slot. Cheap mitigation: refuse new jobs for the same itinerary while one is already 'running'.

No TTL / no cleanup. Each job row keeps the full result blob including the base64 screenshot (~80KB). With ~30 dry-runs per agent per day this collection will grow ~2.5MB/agent/day. Add a TTL index on created_at (e.g. 7 days) or drop the screenshot field after a few hours.

Timeout is hard-coded to 360s. The dry-run completes in ~40s in this environment, so 360 is generous, but the wrapper has no 'cancel the Playwright browser' on TimeoutError — the asyncio task is cancelled but the underlying Chromium child process may linger and consume the global semaphore until the OS reaps it. Wire push_itinerary_to_sofi() to honour cancellation and close the browser on CancelledError.

action_items: (optional) Type the push-to-sofi request body via Pydantic instead of a free dict so dry_run is strictly bool.
(optional) Return 404 instead of 403 for cross-owner job_id reads to avoid existence-leak.
(optional) Add a per-itinerary 'one running job at a time' guard and a TTL on sofi_push_jobs to cap collection growth.
(optional) Wire Chromium cleanup on asyncio.TimeoutError in _run_sofi_push_job_safely.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 4628 lines — the iter-2 / iter-3 split-into-routers advice has compounded again. Sofi push is a self-contained feature (~190 lines, lines 2390-2580) and would be an ideal first carve-out into routers/sofi.py + services/sofi_push.py. Please prioritise the split before adding a fourth integration.

_compute_pricing_totals() duplicates the totals math that already lives in `ItineraryBuilder.totals` (JSX). Two sources of truth for money will eventually drift. Extract a single canonical Python implementation, expose it via /api/itineraries/{id}/totals, and have the frontend consume that so the agent UI and the Sofi push are wire-equivalent by construction.
push_itinerary_to_sofi_endpoint reads `payload.get('dry_run') or False` — see minor issue above. Replace with a Pydantic body model.

_run_sofi_push_job_safely catches bare Exception and stringifies it into the user-facing error field via f'{type(e).__name__}: {e}'. This leaks internal exception types (e.g. 'TimeoutError', 'PlaywrightError', 'MongoError') to the agent UI. Map to user-friendly Spanish messages and log the original for ops.

The 6-min asyncio.wait_for cancels the coroutine but does NOT close the Playwright browser/context if push_itinerary_to_sofi() is mid-await on a Sofi network call. The global semaphore in sofi.py will then be held by a zombie context until the process restarts. Use try/finally inside push_itinerary_to_sofi() to await browser.close() in a shielded block.

sofi_push_jobs documents include the full base64 screenshot (~80KB each) and live forever — no TTL index. Mongo storage will balloon. Either add `db.sofi_push_jobs.create_index('created_at', expireAfterSeconds=604800)` at startup or strip the screenshot after the client has fetched it once.

Authorisation in GET status returns 403 for cross-owner reads but 404 for unknown jobs. This leaks the existence of other agents' jobs to anyone who can guess a job_id prefix (`sofi_` + 12 hex chars — guessable in principle though high entropy). Return 404 uniformly.

_can_access() is called for the POST but NOT explicitly for the GET status endpoint — instead the GET checks only created_by/admin. Edge case: if an itinerary is reassigned to a different agent between the POST and the GET, the original poller can still read the job. Probably fine, but document the policy.

No structured logging around the Sofi job lifecycle — start/success/failure are logged at debug level via the wrapper's logger.exception only on crash. Add INFO-level 'sofi_push.start/done/error' events with job_id + itinerary_id + dry_run + duration_ms for ops visibility.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_sofi_push.py (NEW: 8 cases — 7 pass + 1 explicit skip for real push)
test_credentials: Cookie session_token=3rrWWDXfC1ze9MEHqZzbC0eQK3nq29wClvsJPIMsQhc (eduardo@viajadverdad.com, admin) — supplied in the review request, used as-is.
seed_data_creation: TestAlreadyPushedConflict picks an unpushed itinerary from MongoDB (not TEST_ITN_ID), stamps a fake sofi_trip_id=9999999 + sofi_url + sofi_pushed_at + a `_TEST_sofi_stamp:true` flag, runs the 409 + dry-run-still-allowed assertions, then unsets all four fields in teardown. No data left in the DB after the suite. NO real trip was created in Sofi production — the dry_run=false path is covered only via the DB-stamp simulation. The optional real-push test (test_real_push_creates_sofi_trip_and_stamps_doc) is skipped unless SOFI_RUN_REAL_PUSH=1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sofi push backend fully verified. Local pytest uses MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 + DB_NAME=test_database; the preview URL hits the SAME DB so the 409-conflict fixture (stamps + unstamps a real itinerary doc) works end-to-end. Dry-run takes ~40s here — _poll() has a 6-min ceiling with 3s interval. To execute the real push (#2 of the review request), set SOFI_RUN_REAL_PUSH=1 in the env before pytest and update the itinerary's traveler/notes fields to a clearly synthetic name (the review note suggests 'TEST AUTO PUSH 2026-06-23'). After a real push the itinerary is permanently linked to a Sofi trip — the test does NOT clean up the Sofi-side row. The endpoint and Playwright form-filler all behave correctly; remaining items under backend_issues.minor and critical_code_review_comments are non-blocking hardening suggestions for the main agent.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in the Sofi push integration. All 7 in-scope features pass. Items reported under backend_issues.minor and critical_code_review_comments are robustness / maintainability suggestions, not defects.
